name: Breakneck Ridge

trails_config.name: Breakneck Ridge Loop
trails_config.description: The most famous rock scramble in the NYC metro area. Ascends steep rock ledges immediately from the road. Offers sweeping views of Storm King and the Hudson River.
trails_config.parking_info: Route 9D Parking (or MetroNorth Breakneck Stop), Cold Spring, NY

current_conditions.ai_summary: One of New York's premier peaks, Breakneck Ridge rises to 1260 feet. It is known for its rugged terrain and panoramic vistas.
risk_factors: Check local trail conditions before departing.
Start early to avoid afternoon storms.

generated_description:
The Experience

Standing at 1260 feet, Breakneck Ridge is a standout peak in New York. Known for its rugged and challenging terrain, it offers a quintessential hiking experience.

The Challenge

Hikers often choose the **Breakneck Ridge Loop**, a **3.2 mile** route gaining significant elevation. As part of the **local peak challenge**, it draws peak-baggers from across the region.

Peak Bagging Logic

**Peak Bagging Opportunity:** Consider connecting this hike with **Storm King Mountain**, which is just 1.8 miles away. Linking these peaks is a popular way to maximize your day on the trail.

schema_place.name: Breakneck Ridge
schema_place.description: Breakneck Ridge is a 1,260 foot peak in New York offering hiking trails with scenic views.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to hike Breakneck Ridge?

acceptedAnswer.text: The popular Breakneck Ridge Loop on Breakneck Ridge is 0.3-mile and takes about 0.2 hours round trip. Start early, especially on weekends when parking fills quickly.

mainEntity.name: Is Breakneck Ridge hard to hike?

acceptedAnswer.text: Breakneck Ridge is rated Hard. At 1,260 feet, plan for the terrain and weather typical of New York peaks. Proper footwear and preparation are essential.

mainEntity.name: Do I need a permit to hike Breakneck Ridge?

acceptedAnswer.text: Check the official land manager's site before you go — most trailheads do not require a hiking permit, but parking fees or seasonal access rules may apply.

mainEntity.name: What is the best trail to hike Breakneck Ridge?

acceptedAnswer.text: The most popular route is the Breakneck Ridge Loop, a 0.3-mile hard hike to the Breakneck Ridge summit.

mainEntity.name: When is the best time to hike Breakneck Ridge?

acceptedAnswer.text: Late spring through fall offers the best conditions on Breakneck Ridge. Summer and fall bring the most stable weather; winter hiking requires specialized gear and experience.
